courses.title: CrisisReady 360
courses.subtitle: Complete Crisis Communications Mastery
courses.description: Master the art and science of crisis communications. From the S.P.E.E.D. Framework to social media firestorms, learn how to protect reputations when it matters most.
courses.long_description:
When crisis hits, you have minutes — not hours — to respond. CrisisReady 360 gives you the complete toolkit: a battle-tested framework for rapid response, messaging strategies that hold under pressure, media handling techniques from a veteran spokesman, and social media crisis management that actually works.

Built by Damon Gardenhire, who spent 12 years at the Walton Family Foundation and served as spokesman for INTEGRIS Health, this course distills decades of real-world crisis experience into actionable modules you can deploy immediately.

Whether you're a school superintendent, DA, nonprofit leader, or communications professional — this is the course that makes you the calmest person in the room when everything goes sideways.
courses.instructor_bio: Former spokesman for INTEGRIS Health and 12-year veteran of the Walton Family Foundation. Damon has managed communications through executive crises, public safety incidents, and social media firestorms for organizations across the country.
courses.learning_outcomes: Deploy the S.P.E.E.D. Framework in the first 60 minutes of any crisis|Craft holding statements that protect reputation under pressure|Handle hostile media interviews with confidence and control|Build a Social Threat Index to triage online firestorms|Create pre-built crisis response kits and templates|Lead crisis simulations to pressure-test your team

courses.title: GrantPath
courses.subtitle: Strategic Grant Writing & Funding Acquisition
courses.description: Transform your approach to grants. Learn to identify opportunities, craft winning proposals, and build funder relationships that sustain your mission for years.
courses.long_description:
Most organizations leave millions on the table every year — not because they lack a worthy cause, but because they lack a system. GrantPath gives you the system.

Built from years of helping nonprofits, school districts, and municipalities secure transformative funding, this course takes you from opportunity identification through award management. You'll learn how to read an RFP like a funder reads applications, build narratives that make reviewers say yes, and develop the organizational capacity to sustain a healthy grant portfolio.

Whether you're a development director, executive director, grant writer, or board member who wants to understand the funding landscape — this is your roadmap to sustainable revenue.
courses.instructor_bio: Former spokesman for INTEGRIS Health and 12-year veteran of the Walton Family Foundation. Damon has managed communications through executive crises, public safety incidents, and social media firestorms for organizations across the country.
courses.learning_outcomes: Build a systematic funder pipeline that surfaces the right opportunities|Read RFPs like a funder reads applications|Craft needs statements backed by data-driven storytelling|Design logic models that connect inputs to measurable outcomes|Build budgets that tell a compelling narrative|Develop stewardship practices that turn grants into long-term partnerships

courses.title: Authority Architect
courses.subtitle: Build a Reputation That Opens Doors
courses.description: Position yourself as the go-to expert in your field. Master thought leadership, media presence, and strategic visibility that drives real business results.
courses.long_description:
Expertise is not enough. The world is full of brilliant people nobody has heard of. Authority Architect teaches you to build the kind of reputation that makes opportunities come to you — speaking invitations, media requests, partnership offers, and client inquiries that arrive without cold outreach.

This course draws from Damon Gardenhire's experience building visibility for executives, elected officials, and nonprofit leaders. You'll learn to identify your unique authority position, create content that compounds, master media appearances, and build a personal brand infrastructure that works while you sleep.

Whether you're a consultant, executive, founder, or professional who knows you should be more visible — this is the system that makes thought leadership feel natural, not performative.
courses.instructor_bio: Former spokesman for INTEGRIS Health and 12-year veteran of the Walton Family Foundation. Damon has managed communications through executive crises, public safety incidents, and social media firestorms for organizations across the country.
courses.learning_outcomes: Identify your unique authority position and highest-leverage niche|Build a content engine that compounds visibility over time|Master media appearances and executive presence|Craft an authority statement that positions you as the go-to expert|Develop personal brand infrastructure that works while you sleep|Turn thought leadership into tangible business opportunities

courses.title: AI for the Real World
courses.subtitle: A Leadership Guide to Artificial Intelligence That Actually Works
courses.description: Cut through the AI hype. Learn what actually works, what does not, and how to lead your organization through the AI revolution with confidence and clarity.
courses.long_description:
Every leader is being told they need an AI strategy. Few know where to start. This course strips away the jargon and gives you a practical, real-world framework for understanding and deploying AI in your organization.

Built by Damon Gardenhire — who has spent the last two years building AI-powered tools, advising organizations on AI adoption, and separating signal from noise in the fastest-moving technology landscape in history — this course gives you the vocabulary, the frameworks, and the confidence to lead.

From understanding what large language models actually do, to evaluating vendors, to building your first AI workflow, this is the course every leader wishes they had six months ago.
courses.instructor_bio: Former spokesman for INTEGRIS Health and 12-year veteran of the Walton Family Foundation. Damon has spent the last two years building AI-powered tools and advising organizations on AI adoption, separating signal from noise in the fastest-moving technology landscape in history.

courses.title: On Camera
courses.description: Most people freeze, ramble, or perform when a camera turns on. This course fixes that. Built from hundreds of real media prep sessions, On Camera teaches you to be clear, composed, and compelling whether you are doing a 30-second Instagram reel, a Zoom keynote, a local news hit, or a boardroom presentation on screen. No theory. No fluff. Just the mechanics of how to show up on camera like you belong there.
courses.instructor_bio: Damon Gardenhire is the founder of LINCHPIN, a strategic communications firm that has guided executives, elected officials, and organizations through high-stakes moments for over a decade. A former broadcast journalist and spokesman for Oklahoma's largest health system, he builds the messaging architecture that turns uncertainty into advantage.

courses.title: The Connective Leader
courses.subtitle: How Faith Leaders Become the Infrastructure of Community Change
courses.description: Pastor Phillip Abode has spent decades as more than a spiritual leader — he has been the connective tissue between institutions, families, and movements in Oklahoma City. This course reveals the behind-the-scenes mechanics of how faith leaders build trust across every sector, mobilize communities without formal authority, and become the person everyone calls when the real work needs to get done. This is not a theology course. This is a masterclass in relational infrastructure — the invisible architecture that holds communities together.
courses.instructor_bio: Pastor Phillip Abode has spent decades as the connective tissue between institutions, families, and movements in Oklahoma City. He is the person who gets the call before the press conference, before the vote, before the crisis goes public — building trust across every sector through presence, consistency, and follow-through.

courses.title: The Mayor's Playbook
courses.subtitle: How Strategic Narrative and Bold Initiatives Transform a City
courses.description: Mick Cornett served four terms as Mayor of Oklahoma City during its most dramatic transformation — from a city people drove through to one they moved to. This course is not a memoir. It is an operator's manual for how a mayor actually builds coalitions, passes billion-dollar ballot initiatives, reshapes a city's national narrative, and makes decisions when every option has political consequences. From the MAPS initiatives to putting the city on a diet, Cornett reveals the strategy, timing, and relationships behind the moves that changed Oklahoma City.
courses.instructor_bio: Mick Cornett served four terms as Mayor of Oklahoma City during its most dramatic transformation — from a city people drove through to one they moved to. He championed the MAPS initiatives, put the city on a national diet, and built the coalitions behind billions of dollars in civic investment.

courses.title: Building Schools That Work
courses.subtitle: The Coalition Strategy Behind High-Performing Public Schools
courses.description: Brent Bushey, CEO of FUEL OKC, has spent years at the intersection of business, community, and education — working to ensure that public schools do not just survive but thrive. This course is not about pedagogy. It is about the mechanics of building the coalition of parents, business leaders, educators, and policymakers that a school needs to succeed. Bushey teaches the strategies behind school advocacy, community investment in education, and how business leaders can be genuine partners — not just check writers — in the fight for quality schools.
courses.instructor_bio: Brent Bushey is CEO of FUEL OKC and has spent years at the intersection of business, community, and education in Oklahoma. He builds the coalitions of parents, business leaders, educators, and policymakers that high-performing public schools need to thrive.
courses.instructor_title: CEO of FUEL OKC & Coalition Builder

courses.title: Visionary Ground Game
courses.subtitle: How to Build Institutions That Transform Communities from the Inside Out
courses.description: Chris Brewster founded Santa Fe South Charter Schools — one of the most successful charter networks in Oklahoma — and then turned a dead mall into a community renewal engine with the Crossroads Renewal Project. This course is about the architecture of transformation: how to see what a broken place could become, build the institution that makes it real, and sustain it when everyone says it cannot be done. Brewster teaches the ground-level strategy behind building schools, businesses, and services that work alongside one another for a flourishing community — in neighborhoods that most people have written off.
courses.instructor_bio: Chris Brewster founded Santa Fe South Charter Schools — one of the most successful charter networks in Oklahoma — and then turned a dead mall into a community renewal engine with the Crossroads Renewal Project. He builds institutions in neighborhoods most people have written off.
courses.instructor_title: Founder, Santa Fe South Schools & Crossroads

courses.title: AI for the Rest of Us
courses.subtitle: How Educators, Libraries, and Community Institutions Can Lead the AI Revolution
courses.description: Michael Hanegan is not a Silicon Valley futurist. He is an Oklahoma educator and researcher who has spent years studying how AI will reshape learning, work, and community institutions from the ground up. As founder of Intersections and co-author of Generative AI and Libraries (published by the American Library Association), Hanegan brings a rare perspective: what happens when the most powerful technology in a generation meets the institutions that serve everyone — not just the tech elite. This course is for educators, librarians, community leaders, and anyone who believes that AI should serve Main Street, not just Sand Hill Road.
courses.instructor_bio: Michael Hanegan is an Oklahoma educator, researcher, and founder of Intersections. Co-author of Generative AI and Libraries (American Library Association), he brings a rare ground-level perspective on how AI will reshape learning, work, and community institutions for everyone — not just the tech elite.

courses.title: The Fundraiser's Edge
courses.subtitle: How Political Fundraising, Advocacy Campaigns, and Coalition Finance Actually Work
courses.description: Karma Robinson has raised millions of dollars across political campaigns, policy initiatives, and advocacy organizations in Oklahoma over two decades. She knows where the money comes from, why it moves, and how to build the financial infrastructure that wins campaigns and passes policy. This course pulls back the curtain on the mechanics of political fundraising and advocacy finance — the strategies, relationships, and operational discipline that separate campaigns that win from campaigns that go broke. Whether you are running a ballot initiative, building an advocacy organization, or managing a political campaign, Robinson teaches the financial ground game that makes it all possible.
courses.instructor_bio: Karma Robinson has raised millions of dollars across political campaigns, policy initiatives, and advocacy organizations in Oklahoma over two decades. She knows where the money comes from, why it moves, and how to build the financial infrastructure that wins campaigns and passes policy.

courses.title: Anatomy of Reform
courses.subtitle: How One Senator Took On the Phone Lobby and Won Back Oklahoma Classrooms
courses.description: Sen. Ally Seifried did what seasoned politicians told her was impossible: she passed a statewide cell phone ban in Oklahoma schools — as a freshman senator, against entrenched industry opposition, and with a coalition that crossed every political line. This course is the inside story of Senate Bill 139, from the Jonathan Haidt research that lit the fuse to the floor vote that changed the rules for every school in the state.
courses.instructor_bio: Sen. Ally Seifried is a Republican member of the Oklahoma State Senate representing District 2, covering Rogers and Tulsa counties. A ninth-generation Oklahoman and graduate of Rogers State University, Seifried was elected in 2022 and authored Senate Bill 139, Oklahoma's landmark "bell to bell" cell phone ban for public schools. The bill passed the Senate 30-15, was signed by Governor Kevin Stitt in May 2025, and became mandatory for the 2025-26 school year. Inspired by Jonathan Haidt's research on smartphone harms to adolescent mental health, she built a bipartisan coalition to overcome fierce industry opposition and is now pursuing a permanent ban through SB 1719.
courses.learning_outcomes: Map the complete legislative journey from research to signed law|Build bipartisan coalitions that survive opposition pressure|Navigate committee hearings and floor votes with strategic preparation|Neutralize lobbyist and industry opposition through evidence and allies|Apply Jonathan Haidt's research framework to policy advocacy|Design an enforceable reform strategy that wins over skeptics

courses.title: The Listening System
courses.subtitle: How Human-Centered Design Transforms Schools, Nonprofits, and Public Institutions
courses.description: Every superintendent, executive director, and civic leader says they "listen to stakeholders." Almost none of them do it in a way that actually changes decisions. Dr. Marshall Baker spent two decades inside education systems — as a teacher, principal, and professor — then learned the discipline of user research at a platform used by hundreds of millions of people. This course teaches the methodology he wishes he had had from the beginning.
courses.long_description:
There is a difference between hearing people and actually listening to them — and an even greater difference between listening and letting what you hear reshape your decisions.

Dr. Marshall A. Baker has spent his career on both sides of that divide. As a classroom teacher, building principal, and university professor, he lived inside the systems that shape how communities learn and grow. He saw firsthand how well-intentioned leaders could gather feedback, host town halls, and commission surveys — and still build programs that missed the mark. The data was there. The voices were there. But the methodology to connect them to real institutional change was not.

Then Marshall made a rare pivot. He moved from leading schools to leading user experience research at YouVersion — one of the most widely used digital platforms on Earth. There, he learned a discipline that Silicon Valley takes for granted but public institutions have barely discovered: human-centered design. The practice of treating the people you serve not as an audience to broadcast to, but as the primary source of insight for every decision you make.

The Listening System brings that methodology home — adapted for the realities of school districts, nonprofits, municipal agencies, and community organizations. This is not design thinking as a corporate buzzword. This is design thinking as an equity practice: the belief that those most impacted by a system should help shape it.

You will learn how to conduct generative research that reveals what surveys never capture. How to map the actual journeys of students, parents, clients, and community members through your institution. How to synthesize messy qualitative data into insights that move boards and funders. And how to prototype institutional changes the way product teams prototype features — testing before committing, iterating before scaling.

Marshall teaches with the credibility of someone who has led from inside the system and designed from outside it. His Pahara Fellowship, his PhD from Oklahoma State, and his current school board service in Stillwater, Oklahoma give him a perspective that no pure technologist or pure educator could match.

If you have ever sat in a strategic planning session and wondered whether the plan actually reflected the people it was supposed to serve — this course is for you.
courses.instructor_bio: Dr. Marshall A. Baker is a Pahara Fellow, school board member, and Senior UX Researcher at YouVersion. With a PhD from Oklahoma State University and 20+ years spanning teacher, principal, professor, and systems strategist roles, he brings a rare perspective at the intersection of institutional leadership and human-centered design. He lives in Stillwater, Oklahoma.
courses.instructor_title: Pahara Fellow & Senior UX Researcher, YouVersion
courses.learning_outcomes: Conduct generative user research adapted for schools, nonprofits, and public institutions|Map stakeholder journeys to reveal systemic gaps invisible to traditional feedback methods|Synthesize qualitative data into actionable insights that move boards, funders, and leadership teams|Prototype institutional changes using iterative design methodology|Build participatory design processes that center the voices of those most impacted by the system

The AI Hype Cycle: Signal vs. Noise

4.title: AI That Actually Works
4.description: Practical applications of AI across communications, research, and operations.

lessons.title: AI for Operations and Workflow
lessons.content_html:
Where AI Saves Real Hours

The biggest ROI from AI is not in flashy demos — it is in the unglamorous, repetitive work that consumes your team every week.

Five High-Impact Automation Areas

  1. Document Processing — Contracts, invoices, applications. AI can extract, classify, and route faster than any human team.
  2. Report Generation — Weekly status reports, board summaries, donor updates. Feed the data, get a polished draft in seconds.
  3. Email Triage — Priority routing, auto-drafting responses, flagging urgent items.
  4. Meeting Summaries — Transcribe, summarize, extract action items, distribute to stakeholders automatically.
  5. Data Entry and Reconciliation — The work nobody wants to do is exactly what AI does best.

The 80/20 Rule of AI Automation

Start with the tasks that are high-volume, low-complexity, and well-documented. Those are your quick wins. Save the complex, judgment-heavy tasks for later.

lessons.title: Case Studies: Organizations Getting It Right

4.title: Building Your AI Strategy
4.description: From readiness assessment to 90-day roadmap — build your organization's AI plan.

lessons.title: Evaluating AI Vendors and Tools
lessons.content_html:
The Vendor Evaluation Framework

The AI vendor landscape is noisy, crowded, and changing weekly. Here is how to cut through it.

Five Questions to Ask Every AI Vendor

  1. Where does my data go? — If they cannot answer this clearly, walk away.
  2. What happens when I cancel? — Data portability is non-negotiable.
  3. What is the actual accuracy rate? — Demand benchmarks, not marketing claims.
  4. Who else in my sector uses this? — References from similar organizations matter.
  5. What does the pricing look like at scale? — Many AI tools price per token or per API call. Model your 12-month costs, not just the trial.

Red Flags

  • Claims of 99% accuracy without published benchmarks
  • No option to run on your own infrastructure
  • Contracts longer than 12 months for unproven technology
  • No clear data deletion policy

5.title: The Relational Blueprint
5.description: How faith leaders build trust infrastructure across sectors

lessons.title: Why the Pastor Gets the Call
The Trust Economy: How Relational Capital Works
lessons.content_html:
Understanding Relational Capital

Every leader has positional authority — the title on the door, the org chart, the budget. But relational capital is different. It is earned through presence, consistency, and the willingness to show up when there is nothing to gain.

The Three Currencies of Trust

  • Consistency — You show up whether cameras are rolling or not
  • Confidentiality — People tell you things they would never put in an email
  • Competence — When you say you will handle it, it gets handled

Pastor Abode has built a career on all three. In this lesson, we break down exactly how relational capital compounds over time and why it matters more than any title.

5.title: Mobilizing Without Authority
5.description: Moving communities through influence, not position

lessons.title: The Phone Tree That Moves a City

5.title: The Bridge Builder Playbook
5.description: Connecting institutions, families, and movements when no one else can

6.title: The Art of the Ballot Initiative
6.description: How MAPS passed and what it teaches about selling big ideas to skeptical voters

lessons.title: MAPS: The Initiative That Changed Everything
Building the Coalition Before the Campaign
The Anatomy of a Yes Vote
lessons.content_html:
Why People Vote Yes

Voters do not vote for policy. They vote for a version of their city they want to live in. Every successful ballot initiative tells a story about the future that is more compelling than the cost.

The Four Elements of a Winning Initiative

  • Tangibility — Voters can picture what they are getting (a park, an arena, a streetcar)
  • Fairness — The funding mechanism does not feel punitive to any one group
  • Credibility — The people behind it have a track record of delivering
  • Urgency — There is a reason to do this now, not next year

MAPS had all four. Most failed initiatives are missing at least two.

6.title: Narrative as Infrastructure
6.description: How a mayor reshapes how a city sees itself — and how the world sees it

lessons.title: The City on a Diet: Weaponizing Narrative

6.title: Governing Under Pressure
6.description: Decision-making when every option has political consequences

lessons.title: The Decision Matrix: When Every Option Has Consequences
Managing the Room You Cannot Control

7.title: The Quality Schools Equation
7.description: What actually makes a school work — and it is not what the policy papers say

lessons.title: What a Thriving School Actually Looks Like
The Ecosystem Around the School
lessons.content_html:
Schools Do Not Succeed in Isolation

A school is not just a building with teachers and students. It is the center of a web that includes families, businesses, nonprofits, city government, and faith communities. When that web is strong, the school thrives. When it frays, the school struggles — no matter how good the teachers are.

The Five Pillars of the School Ecosystem

  • Parent Engagement — Not just PTA meetings. Real partnership in student outcomes
  • Business Investment — Mentorship, internships, and workforce pipeline — not just donations
  • Nonprofit Support — After-school programs, mental health, food security
  • Municipal Partnership — Safe routes, infrastructure, zoning that supports families
  • Faith Community — Volunteers, space, and the moral authority to rally support

7.title: Building the Coalition
7.description: How business leaders become genuine partners in education, not just donors

lessons.title: Getting Business Leaders to Show Up (For Real)

7.title: From Advocacy to Action
7.description: Turning community energy into structural change for schools

8.title: Seeing What Others Cannot
8.description: How visionary leaders identify opportunity in places everyone else has abandoned

lessons.title: The Dead Mall and the Dream
Santa Fe South: Building a School From Nothing
The Opportunity Audit
lessons.content_html:
Finding the Diamond in the Rough

Every community has abandoned assets — buildings, institutions, relationships — that most people see as problems. Visionary leaders see them as raw material.

How to Audit Your Community for Hidden Opportunities

  • Physical assets — What buildings, land, or infrastructure is underutilized or abandoned?
  • Human capital — What talent exists in the community that has no platform?
  • Institutional gaps — What services are missing that, if present, would change everything?
  • Narrative gaps — What story does the community tell about itself, and is it true?

Brewster found all four at Crossroads Mall. The building was dead. The community had talent. Schools and services were desperately needed. And the story of South OKC was one of decline — until someone rewrote it.

8.title: Building the Institution
8.description: From charter school to community ecosystem — the mechanics of building something that lasts

8.title: The Crossroads Model
8.description: Turning a dead mall into a hub for schools, businesses, and services that transform a neighborhood

lessons.title: The Crossroads Vision: Schools, Business, and Services Under One Roof

9.title: AI Without the Hype
9.description: Cutting through the noise to understand what AI actually does and does not do

lessons.title: What AI Actually Is (and What It Is Not)
The Tools That Matter Right Now
lessons.content_html:
A Practical Map of the AI Landscape

There are thousands of AI tools. Most of them are wrappers around the same handful of models. Here is what you actually need to know.

The Three Categories That Matter

  • Language Models — ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini. They generate and analyze text. They are the most versatile tools available today.
  • Image and Media Generators — Midjourney, DALL-E, Runway. They create visual and video content. Powerful but narrow.
  • Workflow Automation — Tools that connect AI to your existing systems. This is where the real productivity gains live.

Everything else is either a specialization of these three categories or a marketing gimmick. Hanegan breaks down which tools are worth your time and which are noise.

9.title: AI in the Learning Ecosystem
9.description: How AI reshapes teaching, libraries, and community education — practically, not theoretically

9.title: Building an AI-Ready Institution
9.description: Practical frameworks for integrating AI into your organization without losing what matters

10.title: The Money Map
10.description: Understanding where political and advocacy money comes from and how it moves

lessons.title: Follow the Money: How Political Finance Really Works
The Donor Ecosystem
lessons.content_html:
Understanding Who Gives and Why

Political and advocacy fundraising is not charity. Donors give because they want outcomes — policy changes, candidates elected, issues advanced. Understanding donor motivation is the foundation of every successful fundraising operation.

The Four Types of Political Donors

  • Ideological — They believe in the cause. They give because it aligns with their values.
  • Transactional — They want access, influence, or a seat at the table. The gift is an investment.
  • Social — They give because their peers give. The event, the network, the community matters more than the policy.
  • Legacy — They want to build something that outlasts them. Named programs, endowments, institutional giving.

Every fundraising strategy needs to address all four. Robinson breaks down how to identify, cultivate, and retain each type.

With a PhD from Oklahoma State University and 20+ years spanning teacher, principal, professor, and systems strategist roles, he brings a rare perspective at the intersection of institutional leadership and human-centered design. He lives in Stillwater, Oklahoma.

Karma Robinson has raised millions of dollars across political campaigns, policy initiatives, and advocacy organizations in Oklahoma over two decades. She knows where the money comes from, why it moves, and how to build the financial infrastructure that wins campaigns and passes policy.

Michael Hanegan is an Oklahoma educator, researcher, and founder of Intersections. Co-author of Generative AI and Libraries (American Library Association), he brings a rare ground-level perspective on how AI will reshape learning, work, and community institutions for everyone — not just the tech elite.

Mick Cornett served four terms as Mayor of Oklahoma City during its most dramatic transformation — from a city people drove through to one they moved to. He championed the MAPS initiatives, put the city on a national diet, and built the coalitions behind billions of dollars in civic investment.

Pastor Phillip Abode has spent decades as the connective tissue between institutions, families, and movements in Oklahoma City. He is the person who gets the call before the press conference, before the vote, before the crisis goes public — building trust across every sector through presence, consistency, and follow-through.

Sen. Ally Seifried is a Republican member of the Oklahoma State Senate representing District 2, covering Rogers and Tulsa counties. A ninth-generation Oklahoman and graduate of Rogers State University, Seifried was elected in 2022 and authored Senate Bill 139, Oklahoma's landmark "bell to bell" cell phone ban for public schools. The bill passed the Senate 30-15, was signed by Governor Kevin Stitt in May 2025, and became mandatory for the 2025-26 school year. Inspired by Jonathan Haidt's research on smartphone harms to adolescent mental health, she built a bipartisan coalition to overcome fierce industry opposition and is now pursuing a permanent ban through SB 1719.

site_info.site_tagline: A masterclass platform for doers and thinkers who get things done.
